feat(schedule): cache next_change_at() results Add module-level cache for next_change_at() to avoid recalculating the same result repeatedly. Cache uses vim.inspect for parsed schedule hashing and stores single entry with time_mins, parsed_hash, and result. - Add _next_change_cache module variable - Check cache before computing result - Store computed result in cache - Add 3 tests for caching behavior Task: task-4

Added cache for next_change_at() in schedule.lua. Cache stores single entry keyed by (time_mins, vim.inspect(parsed)) and returns cached result when inputs match. Added 3 tests verifying caching behavior: same inputs return cached, different time recomputes, different parsed schedule recomputes. All 19 tests pass.

feat(core): cache parsed schedule to eliminate redundant preprocessing - Add module-level _schedule_cache with hash, parsed, and default_bg fields - Create get_cached_schedule() helper that computes vim.inspect hash - Replace all 4 schedule.preprocess() calls with cached version: - resolve_theme() - arm_schedule_timer() - needs_system_poll() - reload() (clears cache on each reload) - Add tests verifying preprocess is called once per unique schedule Task: task-2

Added module-level schedule cache in core.lua with get_cached_schedule() helper. Replaced 4 schedule.preprocess() calls with cached version. Cache invalidates when schedule changes and clears on reload(). Added 2 tests verifying preprocess is called only once per unique schedule.

test(state): add atomic write tests and fix escape sequence - Add test verifying temp files are cleaned up after successful save - Add test verifying data integrity with atomic rename pattern - Update persistence issue tests to mark atomic writes as FIXED - Fix Lua escape sequence in temp file pattern matching Task: task-1

Implemented atomic state writes in state.lua using temp file + rename + fsync pattern. Modified save() to write to temp file (state.json.tmp.<pid>.<random>), fsync for durability, then atomic rename to target. Added comprehensive error handling with vim.uv.fs_* detailed messages and temp file cleanup on all error paths. Added 2 new tests: temp file cleanup verification and data integrity test. Updated existing tests to mark atomic writes and error info as FIXED. All 22 state tests pass.

feat(state): add file locking for concurrent access in state.lua Implement POSIX flock-based file locking to prevent corruption when multiple Neovim instances access state.json simultaneously. Features: - _acquire_file_lock(exclusive) helper using flock command - _with_lock(exclusive, fn) wrapper for lock-safe operations - Shared locks for concurrent reads in load() - Exclusive locks for atomic writes in save() - Retry logic with timeout for lock contention - Graceful fallback when flock unavailable (Windows, missing binary) Changes: - lua/colorful-times/state.lua: Add locking helpers and wrap save/load - tests/state_spec.lua: Add 4 tests for file locking behavior Task: task-3

Added POSIX flock-based file locking to state.lua for concurrent multi-instance safety. Implements: _acquire_file_lock() with exclusive/shared modes, _with_lock() wrapper, retry logic with timeout, graceful Windows/fallback handling. Modified save() with exclusive locks and load() with shared locks. Added 4 tests for concurrent access scenarios. 27 tests pass (4 new locking tests + 23 original). 32 failures are from task-5's unimplemented validate_state function.
